basic.q: Choose the correct present tense form: 'Every day, the sun ___ in the east.'

Complete the sentence: 'Monkeys ___ bananas.'

advanced.q: Identify the tense and aspect of the sentence: 'By next month, she will have completed her project.'
Choose the correct verb form for the past perfect tense: 'Before the guests arrived, we ___ dinner.'
Identify the sentence that correctly uses the present perfect tense:
options: I have seen that movie three times.

advanced.q: Complete the sentence using the past continuous tense: 'While I ___ television, the phone rang.'
Choose the correct form for a general universal truth:
options: The planets revolved around the sun.
The planets revolve around the sun.
The planets are revolving around the sun.
The planets will revolve around the sun.

advanced.q: Identify the future continuous tense sentence:
options: At 8 PM tonight, I will study.
At 8 PM tonight, I will be studying.
At 8 PM tonight, I have studied.
At 8 PM tonight, I will have studied.

advanced.q: Fill in the blank with the correct tense sequence: 'She said that she ___ the report the day before.'
Which sentence contains an error in tense consistency?
options: When he entered the room, everyone stood up.
She locked the door and walked away.
He walks to the store and bought some milk.
They were eating dinner when the lights went out.

advanced.q: What is the present perfect continuous form of the verb 'work' for first-person plural ('we')?
Complete the conditional sentence structure: 'If it rains tomorrow, we ___ the match.'
Identify the tense of: 'He had been living in New York for five years before he moved.'
Choose the correct verb phrase: 'By the time you arrive, the train ___.'
Which tense is used to describe an action completed at an unspecified time in the past relative to the present?
Fill in the blank: 'Hardly had she stepped outside when it ___ to pour.'
Identify the grammatical tense error in: 'I am knowing her for a long time.' (Correction choice style)
options: Stative verbs like 'know' are rarely used in continuous tenses; it should be 'I have known'.
It should be in the simple future.

advanced.q: Complete the sentence with the correct subjunctive/past tense in a hypothetical context: 'I wish I ___ how to swim.'
Choose the correct sequence of tenses: 'She promises that she ___ help you tomorrow.'
Identify the sentence that correctly expresses a future arrangement or timetable:
options: The train leaves at 6:00 AM tomorrow.
The train will leave at 6:00 AM tomorrow.
The train is left at 6:00 AM tomorrow.
The train has left at 6:00 AM tomorrow.

advanced.q: Fill in the blank: 'By next year, they ___ married for a decade.'
What is the past participle form of the irregular verb 'choose'?
Identify the tense aspect combination in: 'The children have been playing outside all afternoon.'
Complete the sentence: 'If I ___ about the traffic, I would have taken another route.'
Choose the correct verb form: 'She acts as though she ___ everything.'
Identify the passive voice tense in: 'The ancient manuscript was discovered by archeologists.'
Fill in the blank with the appropriate future-in-the-past form: 'He told me that he ___ join us later.'
Which sentence correctly demonstrates the narrative present tense?
options: Yesterday, I walk into the store and see my friend.
Suddenly, the hero opens the door and confronts the villain.
She will walk into the room yesterday.
They have walked to school tomorrow.

advanced.q: Complete the sentence: 'No sooner had the bell rung ___ the students rushed out.'
Identify the correct verb form for a recurring habit in the past: 'When we were children, we ___ swim in the lake every summer.'
Choose the correct tense for a scientific law or timeless fact:
options: The sun rose in the east.
The sun rises in the east.
The sun had risen in the east.
The sun will rise in the east (historically).

advanced.q: Identify the error type or correct choice: 'She has graduated from college last year.' (Correction choice)
options: Change 'has graduated' to 'graduated' because a specific past time ('last year') is mentioned.
